description: Qoder: move session-boundary commands (finish-work, continue) out of the skill matcher into native `.qoder/commands/` Custom Commands. The 5 auto-trigger workflows stay as `.qoder/skills/`. Aligns Qoder with the command/skill split used by every other peer platform.
changelog:
**Qoder UX fix:**
- feat(qoder): deliver session-boundary commands as native Qoder Custom Commands (`.qoder/commands/trellis-{finish-work,continue}.md`) with YAML frontmatter (`name` + `description`), per docs.qoder.com/en/cli/user-guide/command. Previously these were buried in the skill matcher alongside the 5 auto-trigger workflows, so users had no deterministic way to invoke them — they had to hope their chat wording matched the skill's description. Now they type `/trellis-finish-work` or `/trellis-continue` explicitly.
- chore(qoder): the 5 auto-trigger workflows (brainstorm, before-dev, check, update-spec, break-loop) stay as `.qoder/skills/trellis-{name}/SKILL.md`. `start` is filtered out because Qoder is agent-capable and its SessionStart hook already injects the workflow overview (same rule that applies to every other agent-capable platform).
- chore(shared): new `wrapWithCommandFrontmatter` + `COMMAND_DESCRIPTIONS` registry in `configurators/shared.ts` — short, human-facing, imperative one-liners for the `/` palette, distinct from the long auto-trigger prose in `SKILL_DESCRIPTIONS` (which is written for the skill matcher, not a command list).
- chore(migration): upgraders have their old `.qoder/skills/trellis-{finish-work,continue}/SKILL.md` hash-verified and auto-deleted on `trellis update` (pristine copies removed silently, locally-modified copies land on the standard confirm prompt).

migrations.reason: In 0.5.0-beta.10, session-boundary commands move out of the Qoder skill matcher into native Custom Commands so users can invoke them deterministically as `/trellis-finish-work`.
In 0.5.0-beta.10, session-boundary commands move out of the Qoder skill matcher into native Custom Commands so users can invoke them deterministically as `/trellis-continue`.

notes: Two hashes per file cover Unix (python3) and Windows (python) {{PYTHON_CMD}} variants of the current skill content, which has been stable since 0.5.0-beta.0. Users on older betas (unlikely at this point) whose skill content pre-dates the 2026 breaking-change gate will see a 'Modified by you' confirm prompt and can pick [1] Overwrite to complete the split, or [3] Skip to keep the old skill as-is. The new .qoder/commands/ files are written by the standard configure step on `trellis update`.
